name: Active Inference with Expected Free Energy (Probabilistic Policy Selection)
description: Full active inference agent using expected free energy (EFE) for policy selection. The agent maintains categorical beliefs over discrete hidden states (grid cells × food presence), updates beliefs via Bayesian filtering with a learned likelihood matrix A and deterministic transition matrices B. Actions are evaluated by their expected free energy — decomposed into a pragmatic component (KL-divergence from preferred outcomes, encoding goal-directed behavior) and an epistemic component (negative expected information gain, encoding curiosity/exploration). An interoceptive hunger state modulates preferred outcomes: when hungry, the agent strongly prefers food-related observations. This formulation uniquely captures the exploration–exploitation tradeoff inherent in active inference.

variables.name: observation
variables.description: Discrete observation index encoding the agent's current percept. Observation space: {food_here, food_adjacent, food_visible_far, no_food_visible} × {just_ate, did_not_eat} = 8 observation categories.

variables.name: belief_state
variables.description: Categorical probability distribution over K discrete hidden states. Each hidden state represents a (local_cell_index, food_present_flag) pair in a 5×5 local grid centered on the agent. K = 50 (25 cells × 2 food states).

variables.name: prior_belief
variables.description: Predicted state distribution before incorporating current observation. Computed by propagating posterior through transition model.

variables.name: prediction_error
variables.description: Discrepancy between observed and predicted observation likelihoods. Computed as the log-ratio of actual observation likelihood to predicted observation probability.

variables.name: expected_free_energy
variables.description: Total expected free energy for a candidate action. Lower G means the action is more desirable (leads to preferred outcomes and/or reduces uncertainty).

variables.name: pragmatic_value
variables.description: Pragmatic component of EFE: KL-divergence between predicted observation distribution and preferred outcomes. Measures how far the predicted outcome is from the agent's goals.

variables.name: epistemic_value
variables.description: Epistemic component of EFE: negative expected information gain (mutual information between states and observations under the action). More negative = more informative action.

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Internal homeostatic state tracking energy depletion. Increases each step, reduced by eating. Modulates preferred outcome distribution C — high hunger makes food-related observations strongly preferred.

variables.name: preferred_outcomes
variables.description: Preferred observation distribution encoding what the agent 'wants' to observe. Hunger-modulated: when hungry, probability mass concentrates on food_here observations. When sated, distribution is more uniform (allowing exploration).

variables.name: likelihood_matrix
variables.description: Observation likelihood matrix A[o, k] = P(observation o | hidden state k). Maps hidden states to expected observations. Initialized from grid topology and learned via Hebbian updates.
variables.initial_value: initialized from grid topology: food states → food_here observation, etc.

variables.name: transition_matrices
variables.description: Action-conditioned state transition matrices B_a[k', k] = P(state k' | state k, action a). Deterministic for movement actions (shift local grid center). Identity for stay/eat.
variables.initial_value: deterministic transition matrices derived from grid movement rules

variables.name: action
variables.description: Discrete action selected via softmax over negative expected free energy.

parameters.name: policy_softmax_temperature
parameters.source: Friston (2010), controls exploration–exploitation tradeoff in policy selection

parameters.name: epistemic_pragmatic_weight
parameters.source: Balanced weighting; Friston & Kiebel (2009). omega=0 is pure exploitation, omega=1 is pure exploration.

parameters.name: hunger_accumulation_rate
parameters.source: Petzschner et al. (2021), interoceptive dynamics — hunger increases each step

parameters.name: hunger_reduction_on_eating
parameters.source: Petzschner et al. (2021), homeostatic set-point recovery upon food consumption

parameters.name: homeostatic_setpoint
parameters.source: Zero hunger is ideal — deviations from setpoint drive behavior

parameters.name: num_hidden_states
parameters.source: 5×5 local grid × 2 food states = 50 states for tractable inference

parameters.name: num_observations
parameters.source: 4 food proximity categories × 2 ate states = 8 discrete observations

parameters.name: likelihood_learning_rate
parameters.source: Friston (2010), slow learning rate for generative model parameters

parameters.name: hunger_sigmoid_scale
parameters.source: Petzschner et al. (2021), controls sharpness of hunger-to-preference mapping

rules.description: Observation likelihood (generative model): maps hidden states to observation probabilities via likelihood matrix A.
rules.pseudocode:
# P(o | s_k) = A[o, k]
# A is an N_obs × K matrix, columns sum to 1
# Initialize: for each hidden state k = (cell_idx, food_present):
#   if food_present and cell_idx == center: A[obs_food_here, k] = 0.8
#   if food_present and cell_idx is adjacent: A[obs_food_adjacent, k] = 0.6
#   etc. with remaining probability spread over other observations

rules.description: State transition model: deterministic movement shifts the local grid center. Eat and stay keep the same position but may change food_present state.
rules.pseudocode:
# P(s_k' | s_k, a) = B_a[k', k]
# For movement actions: shift local grid indices by action delta
#   B_move_up[k', k] = 1 if k' is the state at (cell_x, cell_y - 1, food_flag)
# For stay: B_stay = I (identity)
# For eat: if food_present at center cell, transition to food_absent state
#   B_eat[k_no_food, k_food_center] = 1.0 (food consumed)
#   B_eat[k, k] = 1.0 for all other states

rules.description: Bayesian state estimation: posterior is proportional to likelihood times prior (discrete Bayes filter).
rules.pseudocode:
# s_belief[k] ∝ A[o_observed, k] * s_prior[k]
numerator = [A[o_observed][k] * s_prior[k] for k in range(K)]
Z = sum(numerator)
if Z > 1e-10:
    s_belief = [n / Z for n in numerator]
else:
    s_belief = [1.0 / K] * K  # fallback to uniform if degenerate

rules.description: Prior prediction under policy: propagate current posterior through transition model for candidate action.

Preferred outcome distribution: hunger-modulated via sigmoid. When hungry, food-related observations are strongly preferred. When sated, preferences are more uniform (allowing curiosity-driven exploration).
rules.pseudocode:
# sigmoid(x) = 1 / (1 + exp(-x))
food_preference = sigmoid(H / sigmoid_scale)  # approaches 1 when H is high
# Observations 0,1 are food-related (food_here × ate/not_ate)
# Observations 2-7 are non-food
C = [0.0] * N_obs
C[0] = food_preference * 0.5   # food_here, just_ate (most preferred when hungry)
C[1] = food_preference * 0.3   # food_here, did_not_eat
C[2] = food_preference * 0.15  # food_adjacent
C[3] = food_preference * 0.05  # food_visible_far
remaining = 1.0 - sum(C[:4])
for i in range(4, N_obs):
    C[i] = remaining / (N_obs - 4)
# Normalize C
total_C = sum(C)
C = [c / total_C for c in C]

rules.description: Expected free energy — pragmatic component: KL-divergence from predicted observation distribution to preferred outcome distribution. Low pragmatic G means predicted outcomes match goals.
rules.pseudocode:
# Q(o | a) = sum_k A[o, k] * s_prior_next[k]  (predicted observation distribution)
Q_o = [sum(A[o][k] * s_prior_next[k] for k in range(K)) for o in range(N_obs)]
# G_prag = D_KL(Q_o || C) = sum_o Q_o[o] * ln(Q_o[o] / C[o])
G_prag = 0.0
for o in range(N_obs):
    if Q_o[o] > 1e-10:
        G_prag += Q_o[o] * log(Q_o[o] / max(C[o], 1e-10))

rules.description: Expected free energy — epistemic component: negative expected information gain (mutual information between hidden states and observations under the action). More negative means the action is more informative.
Total expected free energy: weighted combination of pragmatic and epistemic components.
Policy (action) selection via softmax over negative expected free energy. Actions with lower EFE are more probable.
Hunger dynamics (interoceptive state): hunger increases each step and is reduced when the agent eats. Models interoceptive prediction errors driving homeostatic behavior.
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if (last_action == 'eat' and reward > 0) else 0
H = clip(H + alpha_H - delta_H * ate, 0.0, 1.0)

rules.description: Likelihood matrix learning: Hebbian-like update of A matrix based on observed state–observation co-occurrences. Slowly improves the generative model over time.

decision_logic.description: Active inference with expected free energy: (1) Encode observation into discrete category. (2) Update hunger state. (3) Bayesian belief update from observation. (4) Compute hunger-modulated preferred outcomes. (5) For each candidate action, predict next state, compute pragmatic and epistemic EFE components, combine into total EFE. (6) Select action via softmax over negative EFE. After action, learn likelihood matrix and propagate beliefs as next prior.
pseudocode: food_here = any(f['x'] == agent_x and f['y'] == agent_y for f in food_sources)
food_adjacent = any(abs(f['x']-agent_x) + abs(f['y']-agent_y) == 1 for f in food_sources)
food_visible = any(abs(f['x']-agent_x) + abs(f['y']-agent_y) <= 5 for f in food_sources)
just_ate = last_result.get('consumed', False) if isinstance(last_result, dict) else False
# 0: food_here + just_ate, 1: food_here + not_ate, 2: food_adjacent + just_ate,
# 3: food_adjacent + not_ate, 4: food_visible + just_ate, 5: food_visible + not_ate,
numerator = [A[o_obs][k] * s_prior[k] for k in range(K)]
s_belief = [n / Z for n in numerator]
C = [c / total_C for c in C]
# Special case: eat when no food believed present
# Sum probability of states where food is at center
food_belief = sum(s_belief[k] for k in food_at_center_states)
Q_o = [sum(A[o][k] * s_prior_next[k] for k in range(K)) for o in range(N_obs)]
Q_o = [max(q, 1e-10) for q in Q_o]  # avoid log(0)
Q_o = [q / Q_sum for q in Q_o]
G_prag = sum(Q_o[o] * log(Q_o[o] / max(C[o], 1e-10)) for o in range(N_obs))
# Step 6: Action selection via softmax over -G (R9)
exp_vals = [exp(beta_G * (v - max_nG)) for v in neg_G]
probs = [e / total for e in exp_vals]
col_sum = sum(A[o][k] for o in range(N_obs))
# Use transition model for the action just taken

perception_to_variables.food_at_current_pos: any(f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'] for f in food_sources)
perception_to_variables.food_adjacent: any(abs(f['x']-x) + abs(f['y']-y) == 1 for f in food_sources)

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 for food consumed); reward used to determine 'ate' flag for hunger update

expected_behaviors.description: Hungry agent seeks food: when H > 0.5, pragmatic EFE dominates and agent moves toward food
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set H=0.8, place food at (7,5), agent at (3,5) → assert agent moves right with high probability over 10 steps

expected_behaviors.description: Sated agent explores: when H ≈ 0, epistemic EFE contributes more and agent visits unvisited areas
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set H=0.0 → run 50 steps → assert agent visits more unique cells than a purely exploitation agent

expected_behaviors.description: Agent eats when on food and hungry
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: place food at agent position, H=0.5 → assert 'eat' selected with probability > 0.8

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger increases without eating and decreases after eating
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 20 steps without food → assert H approaches 1.0; then eat → assert H decreases by delta_H

expected_behaviors.description: Likelihood matrix A improves over time: prediction errors decrease as A is learned
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 200 steps → assert mean prediction error at step 150-200 < mean at step 0-50

expected_behaviors.description: Exploration–exploitation balance: omega parameter controls the tradeoff; omega=0 gives pure goal-seeking, omega=1 gives pure information-seeking
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set omega=0 → agent goes straight to food; set omega=1 → agent explores uniformly despite food presence

expected_behaviors.description: Bayesian belief update correctly incorporates observations: posterior concentrates on states consistent with observation
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: observe food_here → assert posterior probability on food-at-center states > 0.7

references: Friston, K. (2010). The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory? Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2787
Friston, K. & Kiebel, S. (2009). Predictive coding under the free-energy principle. Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B, 364, 1211–1221.
Petzschner, F.H., Garfinkel, S.N., Paulus, M.P., Koch, C., & Khalsa, S.S. (2021). Computational Models of Interoception and Body Regulation. Trends in Neurosciences. DOI: 10.1016/j.tins.2020.09.012
Rao, R.P. & Ballard, D.H. (1999). Predictive coding in the visual cortex. Nature Neuroscience, 2, 79–87.
